channel.title: Microsoft Cloud Platform Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily updates from Azure and Microsoft 365 blogs

item.title: Dev Containers for .NET in VS Code: A Beginner‑Friendly Guide That Actually Works
item.description: What Dev Containers are really about At a high level, Dev Containers let you use a Docker container as your development environment inside VS Code. But the real idea is not “Docker for development”. The real idea is this: Move all environment complexity out of your laptop and into...
item.dc:creator: Debapriya

item.title: Azure Key Vault Auto‑Rotation Practicle Guide
item.description: If you’ve ever been called into a Severity-1 issue because a certificate expired at midnight, you already understand why credential rotation matters. Keys, secrets, and certificates sit quietly in the background and when they expire, applications go dark, trust breaks, and teams scramble. This is...
item.dc:creator: ChandraHundigam_MSFT

item.title: How ACR Artifact Cache Handles Multi-Arch Images: What Gets Cached and When Webhooks Fire
item.description: By Johnson Shi (Senior Product Manager), Toddy Mladenov (Principal PM Manager), Luis Dieguez (Principal SWE Manager), Akash Singhal (Senior Software Engineer), Kiran Challa (Senior Software Engineer), Ren Shao (Senior Software Engineer) Introduction Three of the most common questions we hear from...
item.dc:creator: johnsonshi_msft

item.title: Modernizing Industrial Safety and Inspection with AI-Driven Drone Automation
item.description: In large-scale manufacturing and infrastructure environments, maintaining structural integrity is a continuous operational challenge. Industrial facilities—from automotive plants to energy and infrastructure sites—depend on thousands of structural connection points such as bolts and fasteners to...
item.dc:creator: PeterTHLee

item.title: Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb)
item.description: Azure MCP Server is now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb), enabling one-click installation into Claude Desktop and other MCP-compatible clients. The post Azure MCP Server now available as an MCP Bundle (.mcpb) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Victor Colin Amador

item.title: AI-Powered Downtime Investigation for Azure VMs: Automating Root Cause Analysis
item.description: Co-authors: Jie Su, Abhinav Dua, Mukthar Ahmed, Dhruv Joshi, Dibyendu Mondal In a previous post, we shared how Azure Automated VM Recovery works to minimize virtual machine downtime through a three-stage approach: Detection, Diagnosis, and Mitigation. This post goes one layer deeper into how our...
item.dc:creator: Jon_Andoni_Baranda

item.title: Public Preview: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description: Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN, offering a fully managed solution for backing up and restoring Elastic SAN volumes. This integration helps protect data against accidental deletions, ransomware attacks, and application updates by exporting Elastic SAN volumes to independent Managed Disk...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Backup for Elastic SAN
item.description: Azure Backup now supports Elastic SAN, offering a fully managed solution for backing up and restoring Elastic SAN volumes. This integration helps protect data against accidental deletions, ransomware attacks, and application updates by exporting Elastic SUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Azure...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: WireGuard in‑transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters using Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). This capability enables transparent, node‑level encryption for inter‑node pod traffic using WireGuard’s modern...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available and provides VS Code tooling for Microsoft Agent Framework, including agent creation from templates or via GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to Foundry Agent Service from VS Code.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Hosted agents run in an isolated execution sandbox that provides a dedicated, secure runtime per agent session. Sessions start with a clean environment with no shared state between sessions, no cross-session data leakage, and strong compute boundaries between tenants.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Hosted agents run in an isolated execution sandbox that provides a dedicated, secure runtime per agent session. Sessions start with a clean environment with no shared state between sessions, no cross-session data leakage, and strong compute boundaries betUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Foundry Toolkit for Visual Studio Code (formerly AI Toolkit for VS Code)
item.description: Foundry Toolkit for VS Code is now generally available and provides VS Code tooling for Microsoft Agent Framework, including agent creation from templates or via GitHub Copilot, local test/debug with visualization and traces, and direct deployment to FounUpdate Type: GA, Services: Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: WireGuard In‑Transit Encryption for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: WireGuard in‑transit encryption is now generally available for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters using Azure CNI powered by Cilium and Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS). This capability enables transparent, node‑level encryption for inteUpdate Type: GA, Services: Kubernetes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description: On November 10, 2026, support for .NET 8 (LTS) will end. Your apps that are hosted on App Service will continue to run, but security updates will no longer be available and we'll no longer provide customer service for .NET 8 (LTS). Required action: To avoid potential security vulnerabilities and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Support for .NET 8 (LTS) ends on November 10, 2026—upgrade your apps to .NET 10 (LTS)
item.description: On November 10, 2026, support for .NET 8 (LTS) will end. Your apps that are hosted on App Service will continue to run, but security updates will no longer be available and we'll no longer provide customer service for .NET 8 (LTS). Required action: To avo [Sevice: App Service, Target Date: 01 Nov...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: CRC Protection
item.description: Azure Elastic SAN now supports CRC-32C checksum verification when enabled on the client side for connections to Elastic SAN volumes. You can enable CRC protection when creating an Elastic SAN or enable it on an existing Elastic SAN. Elastic SAN also offers the ability to enforce this error...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you greater flexibility to scale read‑intensive workloads. You can now create read replicas from existing ones, creating up to 30 across two levels of replication. Each source supports up to five read...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Cascading read replicas in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: Cascading read replicas are now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you greater flexibility to scale read‑intensive workloads. You can now create read replicas from existing ones, creating up to 30 across two levels of replicationUpdate Type: GA, Services: Database for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: CRC Protection
item.description: Azure Elastic SAN now supports CRC-32C checksum verification when enabled on the client side for connections to Elastic SAN volumes. You can enable CRC protection when creating an Elastic SAN or enable it on an existing Elastic SAN. Elastic SAN also offerUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Elastic...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Designing a Medallion Framework — A Decision Guide
item.description: Everyone draws the same picture: Bronze → Silver → Gold. Three boxes, three arrows. Done. What that picture hides is the dozen design decisions you have to make inside each box — and the ones you make at the boundaries between them. Get those right and onboarding the 200th table feels like...
item.dc:creator: Subhajit1994

item.title: Enhancing Enterprise AI Deployments with Zero Trust Networking
item.description: Problem Statement Azure OpenAI is publicly accessible by default Azure OpenAI frequently asked questions | Microsoft Learn Any application with API access can call it from anywhere Violates: Enterprise security policies Zero Trust architecture Key principles of the Zero Trust network model...
item.dc:creator: kirankumar_manchiwar04

item.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise – Guidance for Azure Pipelines Customers
item.description: On March 31, 2026, malicious versions of the widely used JavaScript HTTP client library Axios were briefly published to the npm registry as part of a supply chain attack. The affected versions — 1.14.1 and 0.30.4 — included a hidden malicious dependency that executed during installation and...
item.dc:creator: Josef Sin

item.title: Fabric Influencers Spotlight April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of the Fabric Influencers Spotlight, a recurring monthly post here to shine a bright light on the places on the internet where Microsoft MVPs & Fabric Super Users are doing some amazing work on all aspects of Microsoft Fabric. The Microsoft Fabric Community team...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: When RAG Isn’t Enough: Moving from Retrieval to Relationship-Aware Systems in Enterprise AI:
item.description: The Problem In an enterprise AI scenario, the goal was to map structured feature data to relevant sections within large technical documents. At a glance, this appears to be a straightforward semantic matching problem. Initial results using semantic search were promising. However, as the system was...
item.dc:creator: ankitasarkar

item.title: When RAG Isnt Enough: Moving from Retrieval to Relationship-Aware Systems in Enterprise AI:
item.description: The Problem In an enterprise AI scenario, the goal was to map structured feature data to relevant sections within large technical documents. At a glance, this appears to be a straightforward sema...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure AI Services, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: When Anthropic’s Managed Agents Meet Microsoft Hosted Agents
item.description: Let’s start from a real engineering pain point. In their engineering blog Managed Agents, Anthropic describes a sobering observation: while building the agent scaffolding (the “harness”) for Claude Sonnet 4.5, they noticed the model suffered from “context anxiety,” so they added context-reset logic...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: Stop Experimenting, Start Building: AI Apps & Agents Dev Days Has You Covered
item.description: The AI landscape has shifted. The question is no longer “Can we build AI applications?” it’s “Can we build AI applications that actually work in production?” Demos are easy. Reliable, scalable, resilient AI systems that handle real-world complexity? That’s where most teams struggle. If you’re an AI...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Join our free livestream series on hosting agents in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Join us for a new 3‑part livestream series where we deploy AI agents on Microsoft Foundry using Microsoft Agent Framework and LangChain/LangGraph, then level them up with tools, observability, and evals. You'll learn how to: Deploy Python agents to Foundry Hosted agents using the Azure Developer...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: How to Troubleshoot Azure Functions Host Startup Issue
item.description: Table of Contents Overview Understanding How the Host Starts Up Host Startup Sequence Host States Key Settings That Affect Startup Startup Failure Categories Common Causes and Solutions 1. Missing or Invalid FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION 2. Missing or Mismatched FUNCTIONS_WORKER_RUNTIME 3. Storage...
item.dc:creator: vikasgupta5

item.title: Host a Microsoft Build //local host Event in Your City
item.description: Content at Microsoft Build will be grounded in technical depth, developer credibility, and hands-on learning and creation. There are a total of 16 sessions available to build your agenda, including 4 labs and 12 breakout sessions, listed below. Labs are approximately 75 minutes in length, while...
item.dc:creator: GalimahB

item.title: Centralizing Enterprise API Access for Agent-Based Architectures
item.description: Problem Statement When building AI agents or automation solutions, calling enterprise APIs directly often means configuring individual HTTP actions within each agent for every API. While this works for simple scenarios, it quickly becomes repetitive and difficult to manage as complexity grows. The...
item.dc:creator: sbaskaran

item.title: Automatic SSO Takeover in Azure AD B2C Custom Policies
item.description: Why This Matters As organizations modernize authentication, many shift toward Single Sign-On (SSO) using providers like Microsoft EntraID. But if you already have users in Azure AD B2C using local accounts (email + password), the transition isn’t straightforward. You’ll run into: Duplicate...
item.dc:creator: anammalu

item.title: Generally Available: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description: Capacity Autoscaling support is now generally available on Elastic SAN. Customers can avoid the constraints of overprovisioning and manual capacity management by setting a policy to automatically expand SAN Capacity based on usage. Autoscaling provides the ability to specify scaling increments for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Capacity Autoscaling
item.description: Capacity Autoscaling support is now generally available on Elastic SAN. Customers can avoid the constraints of overprovisioning and manual capacity management by setting a policy to automatically expand SAN Capacity based on usage. Autoscaling provides thUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Elastic...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Enabling Agentic Data Governance with Hybrid Cloud Flexibility in Azure
item.description: The “Why” Do you manage data in a complex multi-cloud environment? Are you struggling with data silos, evolving regulations, and the pressure to maintain control and compliance across on-prem and multiple clouds? Do you ever wish an intelligent assistant could help shoulder the load of data...
item.dc:creator: Moaz_Mirza

item.title: LangChain.js for Beginners: A Free Course to Build Agentic AI Apps with JavaScript
item.description: Want to build AI agents with JavaScript that go beyond basic chat completions? Agents that reason, call tools, and pull from knowledge bases on their own? We put together a free, open source course to help you get there. LangChain.js for Beginners is 8 chapters and 70+ runnable TypeScript examples....
item.dc:creator: Yohan Lasorsa

item.title: Single Sign-On (SSO) from Native Apps to Embedded Web Views in Microsoft Entra External ID Native Authentication
item.description: We’re excited to announce the General Availability (GA) of Single Sign-On (SSO) from Native Apps to Embedded Web Views for Microsoft Entra External ID (EEID) Native Authentication. This release marks a major milestone in delivering end-to-end seamless authentication experiences for modern CIAM...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description: Azure Monitor provides a complete set of services for monitoring the health and performance of different layers of your Kubernetes infrastructure and the applications that depend on it.Azure Monitor support for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift with Container...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description: Azure Monitor provides a complete set of services for monitoring the health and performance of different layers of your Kubernetes infrastructure and the applications that depend on it.Azure Monitor support for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShiftUpdate Type: GA, Services: Kubernetes...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Microsoft 365 multi-agent workflow with Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description: Most production AI tasks are too complex for a single agent. You need specialists that collaborate, a destination expert that researches the best places to visit, a budget analyst that breaks down costs, an activities curator that finds the best experiences, all coordinated by an orchestration...
item.dc:creator: Vincent_Giraud

item.title: From Oversharing to Enforcement: A Practical Guide to AI Data Security with Microsoft Purview
item.description: Why AI Changed the Data Security Problem AI does not create entirely new categories of risk—it supercharges existing ones. Traditional data leakage stems from ordinary behavior: sharing a document ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure Purview, Categories: Security
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Nested folders support in shortcut transformations
item.description: Nested folders, shortcut transformations, subfolder processing, OneLake, shortcuts, Lakehouse Data lakes rarely come in flat structures. In practice, data is organized across multi-level folder hierarchies, partitioned by date, region, source system, or business unit. Previously, shortcut...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Monitor your Eventstreams with workspace monitoring
item.description: When something goes wrong in a streaming pipeline, the first question is always the same: what happened, and where? Previously, answering that question for Eventstreams meant finding and interpreting metrics in the Data Insights tab, checking Eventstream components one by one, or opening a support...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Resource instance rules for OneLake in Microsoft Fabric
item.description: As enterprises adopt OneLake as their unified data lake for analytics, securing how external services access data in OneLake becomes increasingly important—especially in environments where public internet access must be tightly controlled. Today, we’re introducing resource instance rules for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Monitor Pipeline: A Modern Approach to Telemetry Ingestion at Scale
item.description: Large-scale observability often fails not because of analytics, but because of ingestion. As environments grow—across regions, clouds, and on‑premises sites—traditional log forwarding architectures struggle with scale, reliability, cost, and security. Azure Monitor Pipeline, now generally...
item.dc:creator: adityakumar60

item.title: How AI Agents Are Turning Threat Intelligence Into Validated Detections
item.description: The promise of AI-assisted cybersecurity has long been hampered by a fundamental measurement problem: how do organizations validate whether an AI agent can actually perform the complex, multi-step work that security analysts do every day? Traditional benchmarks test whether models can recall MITRE...
item.dc:creator: adityakumar60

item.title: Cross-workspace logging for MLflow in Microsoft Fabric: Build MLOps workflows with confidence
item.description: Machine learning teams need more than a great model — they need a reliable way to move that model from experimentation to production. Cross-workspace logging for MLflow in Microsoft Fabric, is a capability that enables you to build end-to-end MLOps workflows using the standard MLflow APIs you...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Introducing the Microsoft Sentinel Training Lab. Hands-On Security Operations in Minutes
item.description: A huge thanks to Paul Kew - this lab wouldn't have been possible without his contributions. Security operations is one of those things that’s hard to learn from slides alone. You need to feel what it’s like to triage a multi-stage incident, tune a noisy detection rule, or trace an attacker pivoting...
item.dc:creator: AndreasKapetaniou

item.title: Lessons Learned from Building a Well-Matching Intelligence Layer
item.description: Key insights from the EDA and Ground Truth journey for solving well identity resolution in the Energy industry—challenges that extend to any entity matching problem across distributed systems. The post Lessons Learned from Building a Well-Matching Intelligence Layer appeared first on ISE Developer...
item.dc:creator: Emmeline Hoops, Oscar Fimbres, Kavitha Sreenath
